title: Asthma Care Quick Reference
description: A comprehensive guide on asthma management, including medication information, patient education, and resources.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: This document provides essential information for healthcare professionals and patients regarding asthma management, focusing on inhaled corticosteroids and long-acting beta-agonists.

subsections.title: Inhaled Corticosteroids (ICS)
subsections.content: ICSs are the cornerstone of asthma treatment for long-term control. They reduce inflammation in the airways and are taken daily.

subsections.title: Long-Acting Beta2-Agonists (LABA)
subsections.content: LABAs are used in combination with ICS for patients who require additional asthma control. They should not be used alone.

subsections.title: Common Questions
subsections.content: Addressing patient concerns about side effects, growth, and safety of ICSs and LABAs.

subsections.title: Proper Use
subsections.content: Emphasizing correct inhaler technique and the importance of rinsing the mouth after ICS use.

sections.title: Resources
sections.content: A list of organizations and websites for further information on asthma management.

author: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute
